commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional gateway/SDK `clientContext` bag, normalizes it, stores it on diagnostic session state, and forwards it to trusted diagnostics listeners as private data on lifecycle events.

PR surface: Source +240, Tests +339, Generated 0, Other +4. Total +583 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a reported bug. Source inspection shows current main lacks the requested `clientContext` path, while the branch adds tests for the intended propagation behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer judgment on the trusted diagnostics privacy boundary and contributor-supplied exact-branch proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: feat(gateway): attribute runs via opt-in clientContext on the trusted diagnostic channel
target.body:
## Summary

Lets a gateway `agent` request attach an opaque, plugin-agnostic `clientContext` bag so an **out-of-process observability plugin** can attribute a run's spans to an upstream identity (e.g. an orchestrator/parent task) that it cannot otherwise see. The bag is seeded once onto the run's diagnostic session state and then forwarded to trusted diagnostic observers on every `session.state` / `message.queued` event the run emits — the same shared-state path that already carries `sessionKey`.

**`clientContext` rides the trusted `privateData` channel, never the public event payload.** The lifecycle events stay byte-for-byte unchanged for existing `onDiagnosticEvent` subscribers; the bag is delivered only to the trusted diagnostics channel that bundled/official diagnostics exporters already receive through `ctx.internalDiagnostics.onEvent` — the same channel the model/tool content-capture path (#91256) uses for its private payloads. No new public subscription API is added: the only plugin-SDK surface change is a `clientContext?` field on the already-exported `DiagnosticEventPrivateData`.

### Why the private channel

`session.state` and `message.queued` are queue/state telemetry. Putting a caller-supplied bag directly on those payloads changes the privacy and cardinality contract for **every** diagnostic subscriber. Instead, the carrying events are still emitted **untrusted** (so they stay visible to `onDiagnosticEvent`, unchanged), while the bag travels in `privateData`. `dispatchDiagnosticEvent` forwards `privateData` to trusted listeners independently of the `trusted` flag, so the private payload reaches the trusted diagnostics grant without ever touching the public stream. Privacy comes from the `privateData` channel, not from flipping the event to trusted (which would make seeded runs vanish from the public stream).

## What changed

- **`src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts`** — `clientContext?` added to `DiagnosticEventPrivateData`; new internal `emitInternalDiagnosticEventWithPrivateData` (public/untrusted emit that still carries `privateData` to trusted listeners). No field added to any public event payload.
- **`src/logging/diagnostic.ts`** — lifecycle emits branch: a seeded run uses the private emit, an unseeded run uses the plain public emit. Seeding/clearing (`setDiagnosticSessionClientContext`) is unchanged; a reused session whose later run supplies no/invalid context clears the stale bag so it is never misattributed.
- **`src/infra/diagnostic-client-context.ts`** — bounded, deep-frozen normalizer: deterministic key ordering, prototype-pollution stripping, whole-bag drop on overflow. The size cap is measured in **UTF-8 bytes** (`Buffer.byteLength`), so multibyte payloads can't slip past a code-unit length check.
- **`src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.ts`** — `SessionState.clientContext` stash bridging seed → emit.
- **`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`** + **`packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agent.ts`** + Swift `GatewayModels` — permissive `clientContext` wire field on the agent request (additive; the schema stays `additionalProperties: false`, no protocol version bump).
- **`packages/sdk`** (`types.ts`, `client.ts`) — `clientContext?: Record<string, unknown>` on `AgentRunParams`, forwarded verbatim by `runs.create`; the SDK never inspects it.
- **`src/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime.ts`** + `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` — unchanged public subpath surface; API baseline hash regenerated for the new `DiagnosticEventPrivateData.clientContext` field.

## Verification

Verified in an isolated worktree rebased onto current `main` (Node 22.22.1):

- `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` — clean across all touched files.
- `diagnostic-client-context.test.ts` + `diagnostic-events.test.ts` — 28 pass.
- `diagnostic-client-context-events.test.ts` + `diagnostic.test.ts` — 73 pass.
- `packages/sdk` `index.test.ts` — `clientContext` forwarding test passes.
- `oxlint` + `oxfmt --check` on touched files — clean.
- `plugin-sdk:api:gen` + `--check` — baseline regenerated, check `OK`.

New tests assert the core invariants directly: a public `onDiagnosticEvent` subscriber receives the lifecycle events **without** any `clientContext` (and `JSON.stringify(publicEvents)` never contains the secret), while the trusted diagnostics channel (`onTrustedInternalDiagnosticEvent`, the same path exposed to exporters via `ctx.internalDiagnostics.onEvent`) receives it via `privateData` on both lifecycle types. The "don't gate on `trusted`" mechanic is covered by a regression test that delivers `privateData` for an *untrusted* emit. The byte-cap test rejects a bag that is under the cap by string length but over it in UTF-8 bytes. The SDK test asserts `clientContext` reaches the agent request verbatim.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: an out-of-process diagnostics plugin can attribute a run's OTel spans to a caller-supplied upstream identity, delivered over the trusted private channel without altering the public lifecycle-event contract.

Real environment tested: live OpenClaw gateway with an out-of-process bridge plugin subscribing to the trusted diagnostics channel; OTel spans exported to Tempo. (This validated the **design** on a local build carrying these same changes; this branch is the clean re-port of that design onto current `main`, verified at the type/unit/lint/baseline level above.)

Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the gateway with these changes, drove (a) a seeded run carrying a `clientContext` bag and (b) an unseeded run; inspected gateway logs and queried Tempo by the seeded attributes.

Evidence after fix: the seeded run produced a root span attributed to the upstream identity (`started root span … agent: <upstream>` → `ended upstream root span`), discoverable in Tempo by the seeded attribution keys; the unseeded run fell back to the plugin's default identity. Public `session.state` / `message.queued` payloads carried no `clientContext`.

Observed result after fix: correct per-run attribution with no double-spans and no change visible to public diagnostic subscribers.

What was not tested: the end-to-end Tempo trace was produced by a local build of this design, not by CI on this exact branch; this branch's proof is the worktree type/unit/lint/baseline runs listed under Verification.
target.author: arniesaha
